definition: A component of a machine that is responsible for moving and controlling a mechanism or system.
The device at the end of a robotic arm, designed to interact with the environment.
The branch of mechanics that describes the motion of points, bodies, and systems of bodies without considering mass.
Light Detection and Ranging; a remote sensing method used to examine the surface of the Earth or measure distances.
The use of data from motion sensors to estimate change in position over time.
The number of independent parameters that define a robot's configuration.
Technology that recreates the sense of touch by applying forces, vibrations, or motions to the user.
An interdisciplinary branch of engineering that focuses on the integration of mechanical, electronic, and electrical engineering systems.
Combining sensory data derived from disparate sources to produce more accurate information.
A rotary or linear actuator that allows for precise control of angular or linear position, velocity, and acceleration.
The computational problem of constructing or updating a map of an unknown environment while keeping track of an agent's location within it.
An approach to the coordination of multi-robot systems, consisting of large numbers of mostly simple physical robots.
Operating a machine or robot at a distance.
Any robot that can move intelligently in its environment without being overseen directly by an operator.
A collaborative robot intended for direct human robot interaction within a shared space.
An unmanned aerial vehicle that can navigate autonomously or be controlled remotely.
A wearable mobile machine that is powered by a system of electric motors, pneumatics, levers, or hydraulics.
A robotic arm used to manipulate materials without direct physical contact by the operator.
The process of breaking down a desired movement task into discrete motions that satisfy movement constraints.
The carrying capacity of a robot or drone.
A robot's ability to sense its own internal state, such as joint angles and motor currents.
An industrial robot whose three principal axes of control are linear and mutually at right angles to each other.
A robot with rotary joints, ranging from simple two-joint structures to systems with ten or more interactive joints.
Selective Compliance Assembly Robot Arm; a type of industrial robot commonly used for assembly applications.
A type of parallel robot consisting of three arms connected to universal joints at the base.
The technology and methods used to provide imaging-based automatic inspection and analysis.
